narrative: Tata Steel is the overall leader for workforce diversity in FY2022-23 if the lens is employee gender representation: it reports the highest female employee share at 8.43%, ahead of SAIL at 6.11%, JSW at 5.78% and Jindal Stainless at 2.89%. However, Tata’s FY2022-23 employee data is flagged as consolidated, including Indian and international operations, so its lead must be interpreted cautiously. For Board diversity, Jindal Stainless is the strongest performer, with 30.00% female Board representation and 3 women directors; JSW and SAIL also report 3 women directors, while Tata reports 2. SAIL is the only company with female KMP representation in FY2022-23, at 1 female KMP or 5.00%. Jindal Stainless is the laggard on employee gender diversity, with the lowest female employee share, but not on Board diversity. Wage-share and wellbeing-cost metrics are not reported for FY2022-23 across all companies, limiting a full workforce-equity view.
